role_title: Senior Lead Program Manager, Technical Programs

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in technical program management
Senior Lead title with multi-year strategy ownership
Comp range $172K-$258K base, consistent with senior IC at a late-stage SaaS company
Expected to define frameworks, drive executive communication, and own perpetual programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in technical program management within a fast-paced engineering environment (ideally SaaS or high-growth tech).
Proven ability to successfully manage a perpetual program with a rolling roadmap
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver results across complex, cross-functional programs.
Strong project management skills with fluency in tools such as Asana, Jira, Linear or Smartsheet.

scope.surface: Critical, core technology platform supporting a key customer vertical (enterprise-facing platform programs)
core_responsibilities: Partner with Engineering and Product to define the multi-year technical strategy and roadmap
Prioritize and program manage strategic, non-functional programs (e.g., technical debt, capacity planning, architecture scaling) and specific enterprise initiatives
Oversee the full lifecycle of platform features, from technical design to implementation
Define clear ways of working, create frameworks for cross-team collaboration, establish content repositories
Serve as the primary communication leader, tailoring information to secure buy-in and consensus across engineering and business leadership
Drive a mindset of operational excellence, prioritizing automation over manual efforts

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($172K-$258K base) and competitive for a senior IC TPM
Explicit encouragement to apply even if not an exact match
Clear ownership scope: long-term health and strategy of a core technology platform
Role has strategic gravitas: multi-year roadmap, executive communication, enterprise narrative
AI fluency and responsible AI use are explicitly valued and supported
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international applicants
Location type not explicitly stated in JD body; 'hybrid' inferred from 10% travel requirement and US-wide salary range
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent — makes differentiation harder for candidates
10+ years hard floor is a significant bar; no equivalent experience path mentioned for this requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Lead', comp range, and 10+ years requirement)
location.type (inferred as hybrid/flexible from '10% travel' and US-wide salary range; not explicitly stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets' and no city listed)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description: B2B SaaS ecommerce marketing platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets')
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated. The JD mentions up to 10% travel and a US-wide salary range suggesting flexible/remote-eligible, but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation is given. Treated as hybrid here.

The '(ideally SaaS or high-growth tech)' qualifier on the 10+ years requirement is parenthetical — could be read as a strong preference (soft) or an implicit hard requirement. Treated as hard with a soft qualifier per JD language.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Treated as unclear.
